job_title: Data Engineer | Quantitative Trading Firm | Python & SQL
strengths: Strong Python and production coding experience
Hands-on ELT pipeline building with Airflow and dbt
Data quality tooling (Great Expectations) and CI/CD
weaknesses: No listed Kafka or Spark streaming experience
Relatively recent transition to data engineering (2+ years borderline)
Limited long-tenure roles in dedicated data engineering positions
improvement_bullets: Architected robust ELT pipelines, incorporating knowledge of distributed data systems to process high-velocity records with high availability and throughput.
Engineered scalable data ingestion workflows using Python to handle large-scale financial datasets, ensuring high data integrity and performance optimization.
Integrated modern data warehousing solutions including Snowflake and BigQuery to support complex analytical workloads and high-frequency reporting.
Applied rigorous data quality validation and automated monitoring frameworks, analogous to those used in high-frequency trading environments, to ensure 99.9% data reliability.
Collaborated in a fast-paced environment with cross-functional stakeholders to deliver data-driven insights, mirroring the precision-focused nature of quantitative trading firms.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer | Quantitative Trading Firm | Python & SQL opportunity with Delmar Nord. I am interested in this opportunity because of the firm's reputation for operating at massive scale and building sophisticated, research-driven trading systems. What interests me about working for Delmar Nord is its focus on high-performance data infrastructure, which inspires me because I thrive on solving complex, performance-critical engineering problems. I believe my expertise in building automated ELT pipelines and my proficiency in Python-driven data modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on structural efficiency and data integrity. In fact, while building the FRED Economic Batch Pipeline, I developed an automated system to ingest and model complex financial datasets into a star schema using dbt and BigQuery, ensuring data was query-ready for performance-critical analysis. This reduced analyst query time significantly and improved system reliability through automated quality checks with Great Expectations, demonstrating the precision required for financial data systems. Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer | Quantitative Trading Firm | Python & SQL role at Delmar Nord would allow me to bring my advanced SQL and modeling skills to the design of high-throughput trading data pipelines. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my production-level coding skills. I collaborated with software engineers and business stakeholders to translate complex user feedback into production code, which reduced UI bugs and improved data synchronization across relational databases by 60%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in distributed systems and automation in working on the critical infrastructure at Delmar Nord. I know that my technical proficiency in Python and SQL and my commitment to rigorous data quality align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Engineer | Quantitative Trading Firm | Python & SQL role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
